title: Microsoft-OpenAI rewrite opens the door for Anthropic and Google - The New Stack
description: OpenAI and Microsoft restructure their partnership, opening the door for OpenAI to work with AWS, Google, and other cloud providers beyond Azure.

title: GitHub moves Copilot to usage-based billing as AI coding costs climb - The New Stack
description: GitHub Copilot moves to usage-based billing on June 1, replacing premium requests with token-based AI credits across Pro, Business and Enterprise plans.

title: Why AI engineering needs old-school discipline - The New Stack
description: Thoughtworks' Nimisha Asthagiri unpacks the agentic AI PoC-to-production gap, why engineering fundamentals are back, and the rise of dark code.

title: "I was tired of explaining it to somebody who was supposed to build it for me": Meet the executives vibe-coding their own tools. - The New Stack
description: CEOs and senior executives are using AI coding tools to build agents, dashboards, and production systems — no developers required, for better or worse.

title: What the 2026 State of Open Source report reveals about digital autonomy - The New Stack
description: The 2026 State of Open Source report highlights the shift toward digital autonomy, balancing vendor freedom with maintenance and security.

title: Why developers are betting on Postgres for AI - The New Stack
description: Learn why developers choose Postgres for AI. See how pgvector and structured data provide the ground truth for reliable AI apps.

title: Can your operations handle your security ambitions? - The New Stack
description: Discover why operational maturity is the foundation of security. Scale your program effectively without creating bottlenecks in your stack.

title: GitHub veteran Brian Douglas launches Paper Compute to fix AI agent infrastructure - The New Stack
description: Brian Douglas and John McBride launch Paper Compute Company to solve AI infrastructure gaps, starting with Tapes and StereOS, bringing open source rigor to agents.

title: The disappearing AI middle class - The New Stack
description: OpenAI and DeepSeek pricing bets have split the market. Here's why the AI middle class is disappearing and how developers must adapt to a new economy.

title: Beyond prompting: How KubeStellar reached 81% PR acceptance with AI agents - The New Stack
description: Learn how KubeStellar scaled AI development with 91% test coverage and a maturity model for autonomous coding agents.

title: The one Slack message that proved our elite engineering team was flying blind - The New Stack
description: A simple Slack message revealed massive multi-cloud blind spots. Learn why a unified infrastructure inventory is your foundation.

title: How to find and unlock the data hidden within videos - The New Stack
description: Unlock video data with hybrid search. Learn to use Vespa and CLIP for efficient video preprocessing and multimodal retrieval.

title: The debugging wars: Cursor 3 takes aim at Claude Code's agentic edge - The New Stack
description: Cursor's new Agents Window brings agentic debugging to a familiar chat interface — but does it close the gap with Claude Code, or just narrow it?

title: Jaeger adopts OpenTelemetry at its core to solve the AI agent observability gap - The New Stack
description: Jaeger v2 uses OpenTelemetry and MCP to trace GenAI pipelines and facilitate collaboration between engineers and AI agents for observability.

title: The real story from OpenAI’s big week is Workspace Agents, not GPT-5.5 - The New Stack
description: GPT-5.5 will get the attention, but Workspace Agents is the launch that could finally turn enterprise AI from scattered experiments into shared infrastructure.

title: Mistral’s Leanstral wants to kill off human-in-the-loop code checks, but is it blowing in the wind? - The New Stack
description: Mistral AI's Leanstral uses formal verification to automate code review — but can mathematical proof replace human judgment in real-world software deployments?

title: Vectors gave us AI search, tensors are going to make it smarter - The New Stack
description: Vectors power AI search, but their flatness limits them. At this Vespa.ai webinar hosted by The New Stack, learn how tensors improve relevance, multimodal search, and future-proof your AI data strategy.

title: “Mythos-like hacking, open to all”: Industry reacts to OpenAI’s GPT 5.5 - The New Stack
description: Community reaction to OpenAI’s latest frontier model, as developers weigh real-world performance, cost, access and security

title: Why Claude needs a real environment to validate cloud-native code - The New Stack
description: Stop burying developers in review. Learn why AI coding agents need real-world environments to validate complex cloud-native applications.

title: Cursor and Chainguard partner to lock down the AI agent supply chain - The New Stack
description: Cursor and Chainguard partner to secure AI-generated code. Integration embeds hardened container images into agentic workflows to stop supply chain attacks.

title: OpenAI's new Privacy Filter runs on your laptop so PII never hits the cloud - The New Stack
description: OpenAI's new Privacy Filter detects and redacts PII in long-form text with a 96% F1 score, runs locally, and handles up to 128,000 tokens in one pass.

title: OpenAI debuts always-on agents to end the friction of manual team handoffs - The New Stack
description: OpenAI debuts shared workspace agents for ChatGPT, a set of tools to automate multi-step team workflows across Slack and internal data systems.

title: OpenAI launches GPT-5.5, calling it "a new class of intelligence" - The New Stack
description: The new model beats Anthropic's Opus 4.7 on most standard benchmarks.

title: Jim Bugwadia on why finding a Kubernetes problem is only half the battle for Kyverno users - The New Stack
description: Jim Bugwadia explains Kyverno’s journey to CNCF graduation and 3 billion downloads. Discover how Kubernetes policy is evolving for AI workloads in 2026.

title: Only 5% of firms are seeing AI ROI. Our May 7 live event will explain how to change those metrics. - The New Stack
description: New data shows only 5% of firms find AI ROI. Join Octopus Deploy on May 7, 2026, to explore research on AI adoption and metrics that actually move the needle.

title: AI shrinkflation: Why Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.7 may be less capable than the model it replaced - The New Stack
description: Claude Opus 4.7 users report self-contradicting responses and degraded performance, raising questions about AI model quality, safety tradeoffs, and shrinkflation.
